whatItIs: In Massachusetts, a name change is a public court record. You can ask the court to impound the record (and related filings) for safety reasons, which keeps it from public view.
actions: When you file, ask the court to impound the record on safety grounds — a legal-aid or LGBTQ legal org can help you make the request.
If your order is already public, ask the clerk about impounding it after the fact.
Then suppress the downstream copies: broker opt-outs, Google 'Results about you', and the web-archive and search-cache removals.
disclaimer: Informational only — not legal advice, and not yet reviewed by a lawyer. Massachusetts practice varies by court and judge and changes over time; verify with the court or a legal-aid org before filing.
